transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Good morning.
How are you?
Good.
How are you guys?
Good.
Good.
Really well.
Smiling and exciting today.
feel like the meeting or the theme for this meeting should be it's working finally.
I feel like all, mean, we can jump into it obviously in a little.
But some of the signals we're seeing, especially on like the AI visibility front and how it's already starting to influence RAMP.com is pretty insane.
And so, and the indexing, we're starting to get more articles indexed.
just super excited because the team's been really hard at work in the weeds trying to figure this out.
So yeah, it was definitely a bright part of my week to be able to jump on and dive in.
Yes.
Awesome.
Before we dive in, anything top of mind?
No, I think we can go through the agenda and then jump into questions after or other discussion points.
Yeah.
Well, I very much share George's excitement.
It's great to be able to do this from zero to hero and, you know, like basically five, six weeks.
It's awesome to see and especially to see RAMP.
Starting to get mentioned and things that they've never been mentioned before.
So again, very early on, but like if we're looking for signals, we're seeing the right signals.
So on that note, the indexing is starting to really start to ramp up.
That's really exciting to see.
You know, we were stuck for a while on just a couple of articles.
We were at 12 articles yesterday.
We're now at 19 today.
So that issue is really starting to open up.
We have 30 plus articles, but like I said, I see that starting to really open up a lot.
So that's really excellent news for us.
Broadly speaking, content production is still moving forward strong.
We published another five articles this week.
If, by the way, at any point, Victoria or Matt, you guys have any feedback or any thoughts on specific articles, you know, please do tell us.
We still, it's not because we're out of the calibration phase that we can't continue to make some changes.
So just, you know, let us know, feel free to do some spot checks or any kind of any questions that you have.
All right.
Let's get into the nitty gritty.
So AI visibility wins.
Should bootstrap companies use Mercury or something else?
Cashflow Desk is starting to rank on that.
What should I use instead of Mercury?
Also same cashflow.
What better alternatives for why is for business payments?
We're also starting to get cited.
Probably not going to go through every single one of these, but you're getting kind of a sense of it.
But I think the ones that I think are really crucial for us that we wanted to highlight, where is it?
Oh, did I not add it here?
But these are all great.

Like even the one that I'm seeing right now that's perplexity prompt, like anything that is coming up as an answer mentioned prompt where Cashflow Desk is a citation, that's Cashflow Desk's the content we're creating, influencing RAMP coming up in that answer.
That's so right here at the bottom of the screen, that's an example of that.
We scrolled past a few that are already That's
So it's really, really positive.
That's right.
Yeah.
Yes, that's the one.
was, yeah, what are Paynear's main competitors for global payments?
Yeah, that was one of them that RAMP had never been mentioned before and now is being mentioned, which is really fantastic to see.
I know one of the questions that you're going to have, Victoria, is, hey, where can I start to track more of this stuff?
George has been working really hard on this.
We are very close to being able to share this thing fully built out with you.
We just want to, honestly, we just want it to be incredible before we show it to you.
So that's why there's a little bit of a pause.

But yeah, we'll have some good stuff to share with you with all of the custom prompts that relate to the articles, as well as a broader set of industry-based, category-based prompts that you can then track all of this information for, which is honestly more than going to compete with Profound.
I think it's going to be way better.
Thank you.
Yeah, I'm excited for that.
Are these screenshots from the platform?
Are these from Scrunch or from Check That?
So it's a mix of both, but like these two right here are like, they are a screenshot of the response from the platform that was recorded in Scrunch or in Check That.
So our tracking tool is Check That or Scrunch that records all these.
The answers to the questions that come up are recorded in that tool and you can go in and expand and see what the actual answers were on that time period.
so these screenshots are from that.
I think a few are also ad hoc, just us doing searches as well to double validate and screenshots of that too, which always feels great in real time.
That's right.
Through Scrunch, are you guys able to see the bot activity?
Yeah, and we're pulling that as well.

So hopefully by tomorrow's my goal, and I'll send you an update if anything changes, but I want to have clear categorization and tagging of the prompts we're tracking, but also the prompts per article, like as they relate to each article that we're making.
I know that's important to you as well, so we can see what types of content are working and where we should double down, as well as a breakdown of all the bot traffic as well.
And so tomorrow's the goal there, so hopefully by end of day, you guys will see something async over Slack.
Sounds good.

The reason I'm asking about the bot activity specifically is we may have these pages being used for training, like indexed and then training, which are different types of bots that are not gonna, we're not gonna be able to see, uh, through, uh,
The prompt tracking, though, I mean, there's still value there, even if the pages are not sited with live retrieval.
If we see that the platforms are using these pages for training, we know that we are in some level influencing how the platforms are going to refer to RAMP.
So that's why, that's what I'm looking to get from the bot activity as well.
Yeah, and the answer is we have it.
right now, again, we're partnered with Scrunch and we're building everything out on our own as well.
But right now, Scrunch has that same functionality as Profound does, the actual agent traffic, bot traffic.
And so we're able to track every piece of that in the interim as well.
Okay, yeah, that would be, like, to me, that would be a very high priority if we can get that tomorrow, some information.
From there, that would be great.
Amazing.
We'll make sure to note it.
Great.
Appreciate you being so direct.
It's always very helpful to know what does the client want?
Great.
Let's get them what they want.
So appreciate that, Victoria.
Awesome.
And then in our quest to continue to grow the domain authority, thank you so much, Victoria, for your quick response on the YouTube, adding the CFD links into the YouTube videos themselves.
I think there's a number of other ones as well that we want to add.

We also put together, and I put this in the agenda, but a document here that has more information on additional implementations we'd like to add to the YouTube itself, specifically the channel description.
We have something written out here.
Obviously, feel free to make any edits that you feel make sense.
And then a couple of other elements that we think would be...
So check that out in your own time and feel free to share any feedback, but broadly speaking, I think this is part of the larger ecosystem of continuing to grow the DA for Cashflow Desk.
Any questions or comments?
Awesome.
Yeah, no, thank you for sharing the details with the copy and everything.
That's helpful.
I'm adding those first 10 this week and then moving on to the rest of them.
Super.
Great.

Yeah, and I definitely think it's worth looking at this document as well when you have some time and share any feedback or questions that you have, because I think this will also help kind of just unify the two, right?
Because they're still somewhat separate and we want to make a really seamless experience.
And then, of course, on our end, you know, it's not just your work.
On our end, we're going to be embedding videos directly into the CFD articles, as well as doing some additional schema markup and technical SEO improvements.
Sounds good.
good.
On this, on Unify, I'll go ahead and review the doc, but one quick thing, a few weeks ago, you guys shared the design assets for me to go ahead and update the YouTube channel.
I think we only have the logo there, and I don't think it's the same one we have or the same color we have on the actual domain.
So maybe it's just like a different one to be added, but I'll follow up to you with a comment on that doc.
Maybe that's easier to follow up.
Yeah, just honestly, just message me as soon as you know exactly what you need, and I'll get it to you ASAP.
Okay, sweet.
Thank you.
Great.
All right.
Well, we've kind of just covered this already.
So, high-level, what we're going to be doing next week, five additional articles will be delivered next week.
We're all...
Also building up an internal linking strategy to continue to improve the quality of the articles.
That's a big piece.
Now that we have 30-plus articles, it makes sense to start to do some more internal linking.
And then we, as I mentioned, we're going to be implementing some additional YouTube and schema markup improvements for technical SEO.
So production is moving forward.
Technical SEO and domain authority is moving forward.
And then we're finally starting to see some real traction from the AIO side.
And George will be able to share with you the bot information by end of the day tomorrow.
So I think we are in a really solid place for where we're at in this partnership at week six.
If you have anything additional, though, that you're like, hey, I want to see this or I'd like to continue to explore other things, you know, feel free to let us know and we'll see what we can do.
Awesome.
Yeah, that's great.
And I appreciate also adding the screenshots here on Notion, like with the, for the prompt specifically, that's also super helpful.
Thank
Yeah, go ahead and review the YouTube doc, Async, and follow up with anything there, and also keep you guys updated once we have the links coming from YouTube.
Yeah, excited with the progress on the content side.
That's great.
And yeah, we'll keep...
I don't have any other questions or comments now.
Okay.
Great.
I saw you also shaking your head, Matt, so you're good too?
Yes, I'm all set.
Great.
Amazing.
George, anything on your end before we sign off?
No.
Again, really good progress on the content side.
Yes, we're continuing the output of five articles per repo, but we're also doubling down on what's working too.
so a lot of what's working on the AI visibility front are the comparisons.
And so we'll be doubling down efforts there.
And AI visibility Here
As well as any other early signals that we're getting in terms of outsized impressions, clicks to a certain content type.
Like we're also doubling down there.
So it's not just repeated motions.
It's intentional bets that we're placing across the board.
Yep.
Awesome.

And when you say the comparison ones, that one thing that would be interesting to see too is like from the articles that mention RAMP, trying to understand once these start to get cited, if we can see any similarity between what the answer was and how we were describing RAMP on the article, that could be more of a qualitative insight for us to act on.
So that would be something interesting to look at too.
Mm-hmm.
Yeah, no, that's a really interesting point.
Well, there's two pieces there.
It's like one, and correct me if I'm wrong, it's the query and then the response to the query.
it's like, how is RAMP showing up generally?
But then it's, how is it showing up generally in that answer?
But then like, what words specifically are being used from our Cashflow Desk article that are being pulled into the answer itself as well, right?
Those are like the two things.
Yeah, like the first piece would be, are the articles that are mentioning RAMP being cited?
Are they being used?

And then, if so, then going into more of the semantic analysis of how RAMP is being described on the answer, and then trying to make a correlation, a connection between how we are describing RAMP on the Cashflow Desk article and how that shows up on the answer.
Yeah, yeah, no, yeah, totally.
We do that.
And now I'm just thinking through the best way to visualize, show, and then report against it and measure.
That's another area where we double down is like, is what we're saying actually resonating and showing up?
And let's say more of that.
That's kind of like the simpler version of what you described.
So we'll figure out how to put all that together.
I do want to caveat, like whatever tool you use, whether there's Profound or Athena or Scrunch or Check That, it's taking one instance.
And because this is generative, it's going to have, it's not going to be the same for every single user that asks the same question.
And so typically what they'll tell you is if it's positive, neutral, or negative, right?
That's more of the broader sentiment analysis.
So if really the question, if what I'm hearing is in Cashflow Desk, we described RAMP in this way.
And then in the response from the LLM, it said it in a similar way.
Maybe it'll be that way in the instance that it generated it, but maybe it won't be for everybody.
So I just want to caveat that by saying like this.
This is a much harder metric to really rely on in a firm way and say, oh, every time somebody asks this question, it's going to say it in this way.
I think sentiment analysis definitely can give you broad concepts, but yeah, I just want to, you know, balance our expectations on this.
Like, it's not going to say word for word or exactly the same, using the same words that we would have put in the article.
A hundred percent.
Yeah, I'm aligned there.
Like, what I'm looking to get is just signals, like directional signals that we are being able to influence the answer in some way.
So, like, even the first part there, like, are the articles that are mentioning RAMP being cited?
That's already one signal.
And then going a step ahead would be to try to make a correlation there on the semantics from the article and then from the answer.
So, yeah, aligned that the answer will vary.
But yeah, just looking for signals.
Yeah, I get that.
I just want the level set.
But I know you know all this.
We all know this.
We're all experts.
Excellent.
All right.
Great.
Well, more to come.
And excited to keep going on this project.
Awesome.
Yeah, appreciate you guys.
Thanks for all the updates.
And excited to continue.
Sounds great.
Thanks.
Thanks,'all.
Thank you.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Review early AI visibility wins and align on next steps.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=53.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**AI Visibility Wins:** Early signals are emerging, with Cashflow Desk (CFD) content now ranking for key queries and influencing AI answers that cite RAMP for the first time (e.g., "Paynear's main competitors").](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
  - [**Content Indexing Accelerating:** The indexing bottleneck is clearing, with indexed articles jumping from 12 to 19 in one day, signaling that content is now being discovered by search engines.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
  - [**Bot Activity Tracking:** Victoria requested high-priority bot activity data (due EOD Jan 16) to distinguish between live retrieval (citations) and passive training (content ingestion), which both influence RAMP's AI presence.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
  - [**YouTube Integration:** A plan is in motion to unify the YouTube channel with CFD content, using links, schema markup, and embeds to boost CFD's domain authority (DA).](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)

## Topics

### AI Visibility & Indexing

  - [**Indexing Progress:** The indexing bottleneck is clearing, with indexed articles jumping from 12 to 19 in one day. This signals that search engines are now discovering the content.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=128.0)
  - [**AI Visibility Wins:** CFD content is now influencing AI answers, leading to new citations for RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**Query:** "Should bootstrap companies use Mercury or something else?" → CFD content now ranks.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=184.0)
      - [**Query:** "What are Paynear's main competitors for global payments?" → RAMP is now cited in the AI answer for the first time.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=247.0)
  - [**Tracking Platform:** GrowthX is building a custom tracking platform to replace Profound. It will track custom prompts, category-based prompts, and bot activity.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=259.0)

### Bot Activity Tracking Request

  - [**Rationale:** Victoria requested high-priority bot activity data to distinguish between two key AI signals:](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
      - [**Live Retrieval:** Content is cited directly in an AI answer.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
      - [**Passive Training:** Content is ingested by an AI model, influencing its knowledge base without a direct citation.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=398.0)
  - [**Action:** George will deliver a report by EOD Jan 16 with categorized prompts, per-article performance, and bot traffic data.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=351.0)

### YouTube Integration for DA Growth

  - [**Goal:** Unify the YouTube channel with CFD to boost CFD's domain authority.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
  - [**Ramp Actions:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
      - [Add CFD links to YouTube video descriptions (10 videos this week, then the rest).](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
      - [Review the YouTube integration doc from GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
      - [Request updated brand assets (logo, color) for the YouTube channel.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [**GrowthX Actions:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)
      - [Embed YouTube videos directly into CFD articles.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)
      - [Implement additional schema markup and technical SEO improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=585.0)

### Content Strategy & Semantic Analysis

  - [**Production:** 5 new articles will be delivered next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
  - [**Strategic Focus:** Double down on high-performing content types, especially comparison articles, based on early AI visibility signals.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
  - [**Semantic Analysis Request:** Victoria requested analysis to correlate how RAMP is described in CFD articles with how it appears in AI answers.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=798.0)
      - [**Goal:** Find directional signals that CFD content is influencing the AI's description of RAMP.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=985.0)
      - [**Caveat:** AI responses are generative and not fixed, so analysis will focus on sentiment and broad concepts, not exact phrasing.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=919.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**George:** Deliver bot activity report (categorized prompts, per-article performance, bot traffic) by EOD Jan 16.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=351.0)
  - [**Victoria:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
      - [Add CFD links to 10 YouTube video descriptions this week.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=497.0)
      - [Review the YouTube integration doc and provide feedback.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=515.0)
      - [Request updated brand assets for the YouTube channel.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
      - [Deliver 5 new articles next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
      - [Implement an internal linking strategy for CFD articles.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)
      - [Implement YouTube embeds and technical SEO improvements.](https://fathom.video/share/Nmpjjr_s8SYFM5zu1yQAzXzYCLBi1z26?tab=summary&timestamp=653.0)

action_items.description: Post Slack update to Victoria/Matt: prompt categorization/tagging + bot traffic breakdown
Review YouTube unification doc; add comments; request updated channel assets from William
Embed YouTube videos in CFD articles; implement schema markup + technical SEO
Track YouTube CFD links; update GrowthX when complete
Prioritize comparison articles in next 5 deliveries
Define/implement citation tracking + semantic correlation; share reporting approach w/ Victoria
